programme.title: According to Jim

programme.title: According to Jim

programme.title: According to Jim

programme.sub-title: He Ain't Heavy, He's Just Irresponsible

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Al buys into a hardware store and takes entrepreneurship a little too far.

programme.title: Home Improvement

programme.title: Home Improvement

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mike teaches Kristin about work/life balance; Vanessa and Mandy school Jen on camping; Ed asks Kyle how to get into heaven.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: After Mike's beloved truck is stolen, the Baxter family and friends gather together for an emotional goodbye.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mike sets Mandy up with Kyle, one of his employees; Kyle accidentally meets Kristin instead; Mike given new responsibilities at work.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Against Mike's wishes, the rest of the family decides to baby-proof the house; Mike thinks Mandy should get a job delivering pizza.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Eric and Donna squabble over their friends following their breakup; Red and Pastor Dave become buddies.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Eric tries to make Donna jealous by flirting with some girls at a party, while Hyde hooks up with an older woman.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Donna finds comfort in Eric's arms after her mother leaves, and Eric's convinced they're back together.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Kitty redecorates the basement after Hyde moves back in with the Formans; Fez takes up ballet to meet girls.

programme.title: Man With a Plan
programme.desc: Adam tries to organize a birthday dinner for Andi, but soon discovers that managing the guests is harder than he thought.

programme.title: Man With a Plan
programme.desc: Adam and Andi lose footage of an intimate moment they accidentally recorded; they must track it down before anyone can see it.

programme.title: Grounded for Life
programme.desc: Sean finds out Claudia has been out all night partying; Claudia finds out Sean left Jimmy and Henry with Eddie.

programme.title: Grounded for Life
programme.desc: Lily resents her ex-boyfriend's ability to move on so quickly after she dumped him; Eddie's stand-up routine flops until he incorporates material from Claudia and Sean's personal life.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Jackie is faced with a difficult decision that brings the family and community together; Becky starts working a new late-night shift at Casita Bonita; Darlene goes to extreme measures to mend her relationship with Harris.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.sub-title: Smoking Penguins and Santa on Santa Action
programme.desc: Dan is disappointed when everyone but Becky forgets about his birthday; Darlene faces another issue; Dan excludes Jackie from the family Christmas Santa photo for mistreating Louise.

programme.desc: Doggy day care; safe bet; hand me a drink.

programme.title: Just for Laughs Gags
programme.desc: Head Massage; Mistaken Identity; Meditation.

programme.title: World's Funniest Videos: Top 10 Countdown

programme.desc: George Carlin's comedy bit "Seven Dirty Words" is the inspiration for a revenge plot when Donna is fired from the radio station for refusing to wear a bikini.

programme.desc: Eric inspires Jackie to take control of her life, after she freezes on camera during her own public access show.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Items reflect 250 years of American art, artifacts, crafts and collectibles; finds include a flag quilt circa 1880, a Tiffany Studios turtleback glass shade circa 1915 and an 1884 Edison light bulb.

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: Opposition in colonial North America to British policies over taxes, land and sovereignty escalates into violent confrontation; war gives 13 colonies a common cause.

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: When tragedy strikes a local family, Dr. Turner's treatment comes under scrutiny; Trixie worries about the future of midwifery in Britain.

programme.title: Odd Squad

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.desc: Stuck without power in the bamboo forests of China, the Wild Kratts must figure out how to save the pandas, who are being turned into stuffed toys by villain Zach Varmitech.

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.desc: After a groundhog predicts more winter weather, Benny is convinced he will have snow on his birthday so he plans a snow-filled birthday party.

programme.desc: The Loops celebrate the day they met Stu; Liana finds crumbs on Louisa's pillow and accuses her of taking the last cookie.

programme.title: Carl the Collector

programme.desc: Lotta competes in the Fuzzytown Music Maker Championship, but the noise on stage becomes too much for her and she leaves; Carl forgets his bottle cap collection at his dad's house.

programme.title: Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood
programme.sub-title: Margaret's Birthday Buddy; Margaret's Birthday Party
programme.desc: As the Tiger family prepares for Margaret's birthday party, Daniel struggles when the attention is not on him; Daniel feels left out at Margaret's birthday party, until he leads Margaret and friends in a panda game and original birthday song.

programme.title: Phoebe & Jay
programme.desc: Phoebe and Jay learn how to sort items; Phoebe and Jay learn how to make food labels, so everyone can participate in the potluck.

programme.desc: Elmo's bike is great, but Abby's bike might be even better; Tamir teaches Elmo how to deal with feeling jealous and to enjoy the things Elmo already has.

programme.desc: Donkey opens a potato stand so she can share her potatoes with her neighbors; Donkey surprises Clyde with a playdate in the sky.

programme.title: Elinor Wonders Why
